description: Fuel quantity flagged by the deterministic batch-median outlier rule.

template_vars.validation_flags: quantity: Quantity 500.00 is more than 5.0x the batch median (95.00).

expected_response.explanation: The recorded quantity of 500.00 L is more than five times the batch median of 95.00 L, well outside the range the deterministic engine treats as normal for this batch.
contributing_factors: quantity far exceeds the batch median
possible bulk purchase or data-entry error
expected_response.suggested_investigation: Confirm the 500.00 L figure against the underlying fuel invoice or delivery note for this site.

description: Zero-consumption utility record flagged by the deterministic zero-quantity rule.

template_vars.validation_flags: quantity: Zero consumption -- verify this site was active during this period.

expected_response.explanation: Zero kWh of electricity consumption was recorded for this billing period, which the deterministic engine flags because it usually indicates either an inactive site or a missing meter read.
contributing_factors: site may have been inactive or vacant
meter read may not have been captured
expected_response.suggested_investigation: Check whether the site was operational during this billing period and confirm the utility meter read was captured correctly.

description: Stale posting date flagged by the deterministic date-age rule -- a regression case covering a different validation flag type than the quantity-based cases above.

template_vars.validation_flags: posting_date: Date is 512 days in the past -- confirm this falls within the intended reporting period.

expected_response.explanation: The posting date on this record is 512 days in the past, well beyond the window the deterministic engine expects for a current reporting period.
contributing_factors: posting date far outside the expected reporting window
expected_response.suggested_investigation: Confirm whether this transaction genuinely belongs to a prior reporting period or whether the posting date was entered incorrectly.
